title: Every Ebike Accessory You Need to Haul as Much as  Possible
gist: A practical guide to maximizing cargo and family hauling capacity on electric bikes, drawing on the author's multi-year Radwagon experience; covers Family Wagon and Grocery Getter configurations with product picks across panniers, baskets, child seats, lighting, and safety accessories—and highlights that most ebike accessories are proprietary to specific brands, creating platform lock-in.

themes.theme: Ebike as car replacement and cargo utility

insights.claim: Cargo ebikes are being used as practical car replacements for urban family trips of 5 miles or less, with the Radwagon capable of safely transporting two children and substantial cargo simultaneously—suggesting durable, non-fad demand for cargo ebike utility beyond fitness or commuting.
insights.speaker: Michael Venutolo-Mantovani
insights.so_what: Cargo ebike as car-replacement is a growing urban mobility use case; watch Rad Power Bikes, Aventon, and Lectric as direct-to-consumer beneficiaries.
insights.locator: When my wife and I bought our first ebike

insights.claim: Aventon's Abound Pannier Set holds nearly 90 pounds of cargo (44 lbs per pannier), sufficient to carry a week's groceries for a family of four—demonstrating that modern cargo ebike accessories have reached practical parity with car trunks for typical retail runs.
insights.speaker: Michael Venutolo-Mantovani
insights.so_what: Cargo capacity parity removes a key adoption barrier; ebike accessory market (panniers, baskets, cargo nets) is a growing attached-revenue stream for bike brands.
insights.locator: the absolutely massive Abound Pannier Set, which can hold nearly 90 pounds

themes.theme: Accessory ecosystem lock-in and brand strategy

insights.claim: Most ebike accessories—front baskets, panniers, cargo systems, and child seats—are proprietary to specific brands and not cross-compatible, creating a platform lock-in dynamic that incentivizes buyers to stay within one brand's ecosystem.
insights.speaker: Michael Venutolo-Mantovani
insights.so_what: Brand lock-in in ebike accessories creates recurring revenue and switching costs; favors established brands with broad proprietary accessory catalogs.
insights.quote: not every ebike brand is compatible with just any set of panniers
insights.locator: not every ebike brand is compatible

insights.claim: Lectric offers an unusually broad accessory range including surfboard add-ons, fishing-rod holders, and hybrid Family Wagon/Grocery Getter configurations with both child seating and large panniers—differentiating on accessory breadth within the direct-to-consumer ebike segment.
insights.speaker: Michael Venutolo-Mantovani
insights.so_what: Lectric's accessory breadth strategy increases average order value and deepens brand moat; a model that Aventon and Rad may need to match.
insights.locator: Lectric, which seems to dominate the ebike accessory game
